{{Quote|Just one more round, friend. Then homeward bound, friend. Don't forget me in your dreams.|Ackmena the bartender, singing to Dratun and a group of other intoxicated aliens|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
'''Dratun''' was a [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] who, around [[1 ABY/Legends|1.5 ABY]], sat drinking with the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bludlow/Legends|Bludlow]] in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]], located in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. When a [[Tatoo system/Legends|system]]-wide curfew was declared by an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (wallscreen)|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Dratun was one of many patrons who refused to leave the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]]. [[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]] the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] responded by serving them all a free round of [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]] and singing the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Goodnight, But Not Goodbye/Legends|Goodnight, But Not Goodbye]]," which prompted Dratun and every other customer to waltz out the front door. The evening's events were [[Life on Tatooine|recorded]] and shown to all members of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Gentlemen, I guess you didn't hear what I said. The Empire has closed us down! I know this sort of thing happens all the time, I… I'm sure it's just temporary, but… fellas, the party is over!|Ackmena, to Dratun and various other cantina patrons|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
[[File:Saynotoackmena.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Dratun and Bludlow refuse to leave Chalmun's cantina.]]
A [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]]<ref name="Holiday Special" /> named Dratun<ref name="Rogues Gallery">{{InsiderCite|144|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: You're Barred!}}</ref> was in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]], a [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] located in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], one evening<ref name="Holiday Special" /> around [[1 ABY/Legends|1.5 ABY]].<ref name="Leland Holiday">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=870#15778940|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-10-12|time=6:13 PM}}</ref> He took a seat at an empty table and, after being served by the<ref name="Holiday Special" /> assistant [[Sosio Kakra]],<ref name="Who's Who" /> he was joined by the<ref name="Holiday Special" /> [[Advozse/Legends|Advozse]] [[Bom Vimdin/Legends|Bom Vimdin]],<ref name="Who's Who" /> who later left and was replaced by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bludlow/Legends|Bludlow]]. As Dratun and Bludlow sat drinking, an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (wallscreen)|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] appeared on the cantina's [[wallscreen]] and announced that a curfew was being imposed on the entire [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]]. When the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] [[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]] attempted to usher everyone out, Dratun was one of several patrons who responded by throwing garbage at her.<ref name="Holiday Special" />

When Ackmena's further appeals failed, Dratun and Bludlow joined in a cantina-wide call for one more round of [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]], which the bartender reluctantly obliged them. She sang the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Goodnight, But Not Goodbye/Legends|Goodnight, But Not Goodbye]]" while serving her intoxicated customers, and when she called for everyone to follow her, Dratun and Bludlow joined in a dancing line and waltzed out the front door. The evening's events were recorded and broadcast throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a program called "[[Life on Tatooine]]," which was required viewing for all members of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. The program was shown with the hope that its viewers would be uplifted by a feeling of superiority over the cantina's lowly denizens.<ref name="Holiday Special" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|How dare you! You stop that!|Ackmena, to Dratun and several others, after being hit with a handful of garbage|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
Dratun initially refused to leave Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina despite the issuance of an Imperial curfew, and he responded to Ackmena's insistence that he depart by throwing garbage in her direction. However, he later exited during a musical number after being served a free drink. Dratun comforted Bludlow after Ackmena deemed the Rodian "[[Heart/Legends|heartless]]." He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] skin and brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="Holiday Special" />

==Equipment==
Dratun wore brown [[Clothing/Legends|robes]]<ref name="Who's Who" /> around 1.5 ABY.<ref name="Leland Holiday" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ackmenas_patrons.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Dratun first appeared in ''The Star Wars Holiday Special'']]
Dratun was first featured in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] as a nameless background [[Alien/Legends|alien]] in the cantina scene of [[1978]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'',<ref name="Holiday Special" /> in which he was played by an uncredited actor.<ref name="cast list">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/cast.htm|text=Cast|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20121205153159}}</ref> Several of the alien masks used in the scene were recycled from the cantina set of [[1977]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|original ''Star Wars'' film]],<ref name="Roll call">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20030502/index.html|text=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases|int=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases}}</ref> which featured two Gotal characters: [[Feltipern Trevagg/Legends|Feltipern Trevagg]] and an unnamed Gotal.<ref name="A New Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref> As Dratun's hair is of a different color than either of those Gotals, ''Star Wars'' authors [[Tim Veekhoven]] and [[Kevin Beentjes]] have postured that his mask was either altered from its appearance in the original film, or created anew.<ref name="Who's Who" /> The Gotal mask used in the original film was created by makeup artist [[Rick Baker]],<ref name="The Beastmaster">{{InsiderCite|126|The Beastmaster!}}</ref> as were all of the costumes created newly for the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} cantina scene. Filming of the latter took twenty-four hours,<ref name="Starlog">[[Starlog (magazine)|''Starlog'']] Number 19, [[February]] [[1979]]: "[[Star Wars]]" Invades TV</ref> and some of the actors in alien suits passed out due to a lack of oxygen.<ref name="Comedy Hour 2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.vanityfair.com/magazine/2008/12/star_wars_special200812?currentPage=2|text=The Han Solo Comedy Hour! (page 2 of 3)|author=Frank DiGiacomo|date=2008-12-22|work=Vanity Fair|archivedate=20100829100918}}</ref>

In the year [[2000]], [[Star Wars Insider 48|''Star Wars Insider'' 48]]{{'s}} edition of the recurring [[Star Wars Insider|''Insider'']] feature "[[The Last Page]]" used a picture of the then-nameless ''Holiday Special'' Gotal to represent Trevagg.<ref name="The Last Page">[[Star Wars Insider 48|''Star Wars Insider'' 48]]</ref> However, Trevagg had already been established as [[Death/Legends|dying]] prior to the events of the ''Holiday Special'' in the [[1995]] short story ''[[Nightlily: The Lovers' Tale]]''.<ref name="Nightlily">{{StoryCite|story=Nightlily: The Lovers' Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> In [[2013]], ''Insider''{{'s}} [[Star Wars Insider 144|144th issue]] confirmed the the distinction between the two characters by naming the ''Holiday Special'' Gotal "Dratun"<ref name="Rogues Gallery" />—an early, discarded concept name of Trevagg's.<ref name="Roll call" /> The fan site [[Starwarsholidayspecial.com]] had already been referring to the ''Holiday Special'' Gotal as "Dratun" for years.<ref name="fan site">{{WebCite|url=http://starwarsholidayspecial.com/characters/dratun.htm|text=Dratun|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20111014091607}}</ref>
